metadata.name: Risk-Based Internal Auditing (RBIA) Methodology
metadata.description: Risk-Based Internal Auditing (RBIA) is a methodology that focuses audit resources on areas of greatest risk to organizational objectives. RBIA aligns the audit plan with organizational risk assessments, prioritizes audit coverage by risk exposure, and ensures internal audit adds maximum value to the organization.
metadata.source_organization: The Institute of Internal Auditors (IIA) / CRSA Institute
metadata.classification_reasoning: RBIA is the globally adopted methodology for modern internal auditing, endorsed by the IIA and required by auditing standards to ensure audit resources are allocated based on risk.
metadata.framework_purpose: To focus internal audit resources on areas of greatest risk, ensuring audit work is aligned with organizational risk exposure and providing maximum assurance value.
metadata.framework_scope: All internal audit functions seeking to develop and execute risk-based audit plans aligned with organizational risk management frameworks.
framework_objectives: Align audit plan with organizational risk exposure
Prioritize audit resources on highest risk areas
Integrate audit with enterprise risk management
Provide risk-based assurance to the board
Maximize internal audit value and coverage efficiency
metadata.target_audience: Chief Audit Executives, Internal Audit Managers, Risk Managers, Audit Committees, and organizations implementing or improving internal audit functions
adoption_approach: Develop and maintain the audit universe
Conduct annual risk assessment of the audit universe
Monitor and report on audit plan completion
Update risk assessment based on changing risk landscape

controls.title: Audit Universe Development
controls.description: The internal audit function develops and maintains a comprehensive audit universe encompassing all auditable units, processes, and systems.
controls.full_text: The CAE develops an audit universe that includes all auditable entities, business processes, IT systems, regulatory requirements, and significant projects. The universe is organized by business area, risk category, or regulatory domain. The audit universe is reviewed and updated at least annually to reflect organizational changes.

controls.title: Audit Universe Maintenance
controls.description: The audit universe is maintained to reflect current organizational structure, processes, and risk landscape.
controls.full_text: The audit universe is reviewed at least annually and updated for organizational changes, new business activities, regulatory changes, and emerging risks. Additions and deletions are documented with rationale. Senior management and the audit committee are informed of significant changes to the universe.

controls.title: Risk Assessment Methodology
controls.description: The internal audit function applies a documented, consistent methodology for assessing risk across the audit universe.
controls.full_text: The CAE develops and documents a risk assessment methodology that defines risk factors, scoring criteria, and risk weighting. Common factors include inherent risk (business complexity, external threats), control environment quality, management quality, regulatory exposure, financial materiality, and strategic importance. The methodology ensures consistent and defensible risk prioritization.

controls.title: Annual Risk Assessment Execution
controls.description: The internal audit function conducts an annual risk assessment of the audit universe to prioritize audit coverage.
controls.full_text: The CAE conducts a comprehensive risk assessment of each auditable entity in the universe. Risk assessments incorporate input from management, the board, external auditors, regulators, and the internal audit team. Risk factors are scored and aggregated to produce an overall risk rating that drives audit prioritization.
evidence_requirements: Risk ratings for all auditable entities
Risk heat map or prioritization matrix

controls.title: ERM Alignment
controls.description: The internal audit risk assessment is aligned with the organization's enterprise risk management process.
controls.full_text: The CAE coordinates the audit risk assessment with the organization's ERM process. Audit risk ratings reflect the organizational risk register. Areas identified as high risk by ERM receive priority audit coverage. The CAE attends risk committee meetings and reviews ERM reporting.
evidence_requirements: Audit risk assessment vs. ERM comparison

controls.title: Risk-Based Audit Plan Development
controls.description: The annual audit plan is developed based on risk assessment results, allocating audit resources to highest risk areas.
controls.full_text: The CAE translates risk assessment results into an annual audit plan. High-risk areas receive priority scheduling and greater resource allocation. The plan balances risk-based priorities with regulatory requirements, management requests, and coverage of all significant areas over a multi-year cycle. Resource requirements (staff, time, budget) are estimated for each engagement.

controls.title: Audit Plan Approval
controls.description: The risk-based audit plan is approved by senior management and the audit committee, with the risk assessment supporting the prioritization decisions.
controls.full_text: The CAE presents the risk-based audit plan to senior management and the audit committee for approval. The presentation explains how risk assessment drives prioritization, resource requirements, and coverage decisions. The audit committee approves the plan and any subsequent material changes.

controls.title: Dynamic Audit Plan Updates
controls.description: The audit plan is updated dynamically during the year to reflect changes in the risk environment.
controls.full_text: The CAE monitors the risk environment throughout the year and adjusts the audit plan as needed. Material changes in risk — including significant business changes, regulatory changes, incidents, or management requests — may trigger plan updates. Changes are communicated to and approved by the audit committee.
evidence_requirements: Audit committee communications on plan changes

controls.title: Risk-Based Engagement Scoping
controls.description: Each audit engagement is scoped based on risk assessment to focus on the most significant risks within the audit area.
controls.full_text: For each engagement, the auditor conducts a preliminary risk assessment to identify the key risks within scope. The risk assessment informs the engagement objectives, testing focus, and sampling approach. Higher-risk areas receive more intensive testing and deeper substantive procedures.

controls.title: Control Design and Operating Effectiveness
controls.description: Auditors evaluate both the design adequacy and operating effectiveness of controls over identified risks.
controls.full_text: Auditors assess whether controls are appropriately designed to mitigate identified risks and whether they are operating effectively over the period under review. Design evaluation precedes operating effectiveness testing. Results are documented in the risk and control matrix with clear linkage to risks and conclusions.

controls.title: Working Paper Documentation
controls.description: Audit working papers document procedures performed, evidence gathered, and conclusions reached for each engagement.
controls.full_text: Auditors prepare and maintain working papers that document audit procedures, evidence collected, analysis performed, and conclusions. Working papers link back to the work program and RCM. They are reviewed by the in-charge auditor and manager before the report is issued. Working papers are retained per the document retention policy.

controls.title: Risk-Based Findings and Recommendations
controls.description: Audit findings are reported with clear linkage to risks and business impact, and recommendations are actionable and risk-prioritized.
controls.full_text: Audit reports communicate findings in the context of the risks they represent. Each finding includes the condition (what is), criteria (what should be), cause (why), risk/impact (what could happen), and recommendation (what should be done). Findings are rated by risk severity. Recommendations are specific, actionable, and prioritized by risk.

controls.title: Finding Follow-Up and Closure
controls.description: Audit follows up on management's implementation of agreed recommendations to ensure risk remediation.
controls.full_text: The CAE establishes a follow-up process to verify that management has effectively implemented agreed action plans. Follow-up is risk-prioritized, with critical findings receiving earlier and more intensive follow-up. Results are reported to the audit committee. Unresolved issues are escalated appropriately.
evidence_requirements: Audit committee reporting on open items

controls.title: Risk-Focused Board Reporting
controls.description: The CAE provides risk-focused reporting to the board, including overall risk coverage, significant risks identified, and the residual risk posture.
controls.full_text: The CAE provides periodic risk-focused reports to the board and audit committee. Reports include audit plan progress, significant findings and emerging risks, assessment of the overall control environment, and the residual risk profile based on audit work. The CAE communicates any limitations on audit scope or resources.
evidence_requirements: Annual CAE report to audit committee

controls.title: RBIA Quality Assurance
controls.description: The internal audit function maintains a quality assurance and improvement program that validates the effectiveness of the RBIA approach.
controls.full_text: The quality assurance program evaluates whether the risk assessment is comprehensive and current, the audit plan reflects risk priorities, engagement execution meets professional standards, and reporting is timely and effective. Internal and external quality assessments measure RBIA effectiveness.

controls.title: Data Analytics in RBIA
controls.description: The internal audit function leverages data analytics to enhance risk assessment and engagement effectiveness.
controls.full_text: Internal audit uses data analytics to improve risk identification, expand testing coverage, and increase audit efficiency. Analytics are used in the risk assessment (trend analysis, anomaly detection) and in individual engagements (full population testing, continuous monitoring). Technology skills are developed within the audit team.

controls.title: RBIA Continuous Improvement
controls.description: The internal audit function continuously improves its RBIA approach based on internal feedback, external assessments, and industry developments.
controls.full_text: The CAE implements a continuous improvement program for the RBIA methodology. Improvements are based on QAIP results, staff feedback, benchmark data from industry surveys, and external quality assessment recommendations. The board is informed of significant improvements.

controls.title: Stakeholder Satisfaction Measurement
controls.description: The internal audit function measures and acts on stakeholder satisfaction to ensure audit services meet organizational needs.
controls.full_text: The CAE measures satisfaction with internal audit services from the board, management, and other stakeholders. Satisfaction surveys are conducted after engagements and annually. Results are analyzed and used to improve audit processes, communication, and relationships. Results are reported in the QAIP.
